summary: The Alaska Police Standards Council voted down a regulation change that would allow the state to hire corrections officers as young as 18 years old — the current minimum age for hire is 21 years old.

title: ‘Widespread’ sexual and gender-based crimes committed during Hamas attack, Israeli officials say
summary: A new report by the Israeli organization Physicians for Human Rights says the atrocities committed by Hamas were intended to be utilized as a psychological weapon.

title: Charges reduced for Alaska Airlines pilot accused of attempting to cut plane’s engines
summary: The pilot, Joseph Emerson, faces a separate charge of interfering with a flight crew in federal court.

title: House Speaker Johnson is insisting on sweeping border security changes in a deal for Ukraine aid
summary: Biden is pushing a reluctant Congress to approve the military, economic and humanitarian aid package, but the injection of border security into the negotiations has made progress difficult.

title: CVS plans to overhaul how drugs are priced
summary: The pharmacy giant says its new pricing formulas will better represent the “true net cost” of medications.

title: Lawmakers question Harvard, Penn and MIT presidents on antisemitism
summary: Three university presidents, called to Capitol Hill, are being pushed to do more to protect Jewish communities after the outbreak of war in Israel and Gaza.

title: Man believed to have fired shots at officers before Virginia duplex exploded is dead, police say
summary: Fifty-six-year-old James Yoo was identified by Arlington County police as the the owner of the duplex who fired a “flare-type gun” from inside more than 30 times.

title: Math scores for U.S. students hit all-time low on international exam
summary: Despite the low math performance, U.S. students performed better relative to their peers than in past years.

title: In rare action against Israel, Biden administration says extremist West Bank settlers will be barred from U.S.
summary: The State Department says it will impose travel bans on extremist Jewish settlers implicated in a rash of attacks on Palestinians in the occupied West Bank.

title: Man dies at Mat-Su prison after more than 10 years in custody
summary: Artemy Arthur Hapoff’s death had been anticipated by medical personnel, the Alaska Department of Corrections said.

title: Alaska to buy Hawaiian: Here’s what we know about miles and perks
summary: The future remains uncertain for Alaska’s companion fare, which offers cardholders the opportunity to book one ticket at $99 plus tax and fees annually when they are booking for themselves.

title: Alaska Air was ready to grow. Hawaiian hit hard times. An airline match is made.
summary: Hawaiian had a precipitous drop in travel during the COVID-19 pandemic and still hasn’t recovered.

title: Liz Cheney, outspoken Republican Trump critic, weighs third-party presidential run
summary: Cheney, who is now professor of practice with the University of Virginia Center for Politics, receded from the spotlight for much of this year after her prominent role in a congressional panel that investigated the Jan. 6, 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol by a pro-Trump mob.

title: Oil, gas and coal interests swarm global climate summit in Dubai
summary: More than 2,400 people with ties to the fossil fuel industry are registered to attend COP28, according to a review of U.N. data.

title: Double mass shootings over weekend set grim U.S. record
summary: In 2023, the country has experienced 38 mass shootings in which four or more people were killed, the most of any year since at least 2006.

title: Proposed merger of Alaska and Hawaiian airlines a promising fit that should improve air travel for residents of each state, observers say
summary: The deal could establish Honolulu and Seattle as an Alaska Airlines’ launch pad for new international markets, and could boost mileage redemption opportunities for Alaskans, observers said.

title: Four GOP presidential candidates qualify for fourth primary debate
summary: Former United Nations ambassador Nikki Haley, Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis, former New Jersey Gov. Chris Christie, and tech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy will face off on Dec. 6 in the fourth GOP presidential primary debate.

title: Anchorage Assembly to consider allowing drive-thrus, free samples at marijuana retailers
summary: The Anchorage Assembly on Tuesday is considering a pair of ordinance that would change city regulations on marijuana retailers, allowing drive-thru and walk-up windows, internet and phone sales and samples for customers.

title: New complaint alleges Anchorage church illegally aided campaign against ranked-choice voting
summary: Ranked choice voting advocates are asking the state campaign ethics watchdog to pause signature gathering for an effort to repeal Alaska’s voting system.

title: Lawsuit contends Mat-Su school district illegally isolates and restrains disabled students
summary: The plaintiffs, parents of an elementary school student, want the district to immediately change its disabled student isolation and restraint practices.

title: Renewables are proving no match for the world’s appetite for fossil fuels
summary: Even as renewable energy projects and electric vehicles take off, a new analysis by the Global Carbon Project now shows that global greenhouse emissions will grow 1.1% this year.

title: Young Palestinian prisoners describe harsh treatment in Israeli jails
summary: Teenager Noah Bseso says he was held in solitary confinement and given little more than bread some days, part of a crackdown in Israeli jails after Oct. 7.

title: ‘Everybody’s daughter’: The rape victim behind Kentucky’s viral abortion ad
summary: Hadley Duvall’s ad about abortion rights helped Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear win reelection — and she’s ready to campaign again in 2024.

title: 11 holiday cookie recipes to savor and share this season
summary: The Washington Post scoured the country for unique holiday cookies featuring chocolate, spices, fruit and more.

title: Global journalist group says Israel-Hamas conflict is a war beyond compare for media deaths
summary: About 60 journalists or media workers have been killed since the start of the war, already close to the same number of journalists killed during the entire Vietnam War half a century ago.

title: Funding gap looms for Alaska’s domestic violence programs, but need for services is as high as ever
summary: A major source of funding for Alaska’s domestic violence response has decreased significantly in the past five years, leaving a multimillion-dollar hole in the budget for services.

title: Ex-ambassador charged with serving as secret agent for Cuba’s intelligence services for decades
summary: A former American diplomatwho served as U.S. ambassador to Bolivia has been charged with servingas a covert agent for Cuba’s intelligence services dating back decades,the Justice Department said Monday.

title: Florida Republican chairman won’t resign over rape allegation, saying he is innocent
summary: The chair of Florida’s Republican Party says hewill not resign over a woman’s allegation that he raped her, saying inan email to supporters that he is innocent. He did not address anyspecifics of the accusation that has roiled the state’s conservativepolitics.

title: Ships face Houthi-claimed attack in Red Sea as officials say a US warship also fires in self-defense
summary: Commercial ships came under attack Sundayby drones and missiles in the Red Sea and a U.S. warship there openedfire in self-defense as part of an hourslong assault claimed by Yemen’sHouthi rebels, officials said.

title: Nonprofit, with Anchorage city support, aims to create 300-acre conservation park near Potter Marsh
summary: For years, South Anchorage residents have used informal trails that run through the undeveloped area — land also critical to Potter Marsh’s thriving wildlife habitat.

title: Wayne and Wanda: The current housing market is keeping my relationship from progressing to the next level
summary: Wayne and Wanda realize finding housing together can be a major challenge for couples and have some tips on how to troubleshoot the situation.

title: A Spanish region with a diverse and fascinating history, Andalusia is worth a trip for a curious traveler
summary: The area changed hands multiple times over the centuries and still holds Roman, Muslim and Christian influences.

title: Authorities identify suspect in killing of 3 homeless men in Los Angeles
summary: A Los Angeles man already in custody in connection withanother shooting investigation has been identified as the suspect inthree recent killings of homeless men, police said Saturday.

title: Trump pardoned them. Now they’re helping him return to power.
summary: President Trump’s unprecedented use of pardon power to benefit political and financial backers is paying dividends as he seeks to return to the White House.

title: Trump attempts to spin anti-democracy, authoritarian criticism against Biden
summary: Former president Trump sought to turn the tables on rising alarms from Democrats and some Republicans that Trump’s return to power would imperil free elections and civil liberties.

title: NOAA Fisheries releases more information about ‘high level’ of killer whales caught this year by Alaska trawl fleet
summary: Six killer whales caught in trawl net gear this year in waters off Alaska died as a result of their entanglement, while a seventh orca was seriously injured, the agency reported.

title: Police raid Moscow gay bars after a Supreme Court ruling labeled LGBTQ+ movement ‘extremist’
summary: Russian security forces raided gay clubs and bars across Moscow Friday night, less than 48 hours after the country’s top court banned what it called the “global LGBTQ+ movement” as an extremist organization.

title: Dear Therapist: I don’t want to see my mom this Christmas
summary: Dear Therapist: I don’t want to see my mom this Christmas

title: With nation’s highest rate of chronic absenteeism, Alaska school districts explore causes — and solutions
summary: Education officials said potential factors include changed attitudes within school communities, a shift in habits and how families address student illness, and more remote learning flexibility.

title: US targets oil and natural gas industry’s role in global warming with new rule on methane emissions
summary: The Biden administration on Saturday issued a final ruleaimed at reducing methane emissions, targeting the U.S. oil and naturalgas industry for its role in global warming as President Joe Bidenseeks to advance his climate legacy.

title: A trans girl played volleyball. Now her Florida school is under investigation.
summary: An investigation under a 2021 Florida law banning trans girls from playing on girls’ school sports teams has thrust a public high school into turmoil.

title: Miss Manners: The mom of my daughter’s friend keeps comparing our kids. I don’t like it.
summary: Advice from Judith Martin, Nicholas Ivor Martin and Jacobina Martin.

title: Dear Annie: My dad’s applying tough love to my brother. Is it the right call?
summary: Advice from Annie Lane.

title: Ask Amy: Our granddaughter is posting videos on TikTok of us and our pets
summary: Advice from Amy Dickinson.

title: Ask Amy: We need help calming our family drama before it turns into all-out war
summary: Advice from Amy Dickinson.

title: Dear Annie: The woman my husband had an affair with may be our daughter’steacher
summary: Advice from Annie Lane.

title: Japan expresses concern about Ospreys continuing to fly as some crash details emerge
summary: Japan’s top government spokesperson expressed concern that the U.S. military is continuing to fly Osprey aircraft in the country without providing adequate information about Wednesday’s fatal crash.

title: Trump attorney: Georgia trial during campaign would be ‘election interference’ if he is GOP nominee
summary: An attorney for former President Donald Trump said it would amount to “election interference” if his client is the Republican nominee for president while on trial in Georgia in the months running up to the general election.

title: Inmate accused of stabbing Derek Chauvin 22 times charged with attempted murder, prosecutors say
summary: Federal prosecutors said the man, a former gang member and FBI informant, said he would’ve killed Chauvin had correctional officers not responded so quickly.

title: Dear Annie: The woman my husband had an affair with may now be our daughter’s teacher
summary: Advice from Annie Lane.

title: Nearly 2 months into the war, many Israelis have no idea if their relatives are dead or alive
summary: Some of the bodies of those who died were so badly burned in fires or explosions that there’s little left to identify, while others who might be alive still haven’t been traced.

title: Sandra Day O’Connor, first woman on the U.S. Supreme Court, has died at age 93
summary: O’Connor was appointed by President Reagan in 1981 and became an unwavering voice of moderate conservatism. She retired in 2006.

title: Searchers find body of missing man 10 days after Wrangell landslide
summary: A 12-year-old boy was the last person still missing Friday as crews continued “reactive searching,” Alaska State Troopers said.

title: Lawsuits against Trump over Jan. 6 Capitol riot can move forward, appeals court says
summary: A three-judge panel of the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit rejected Donald Trump’s sweeping claims that presidential immunity shields him from liability in lawsuits brought by police officers and Democratic lawmakers.

title: Ask Amy: My 3rd-grade daughter is being bullied
summary: Advice from Amy Dickinson.

title: Dear Annie: ‘Welcome to adulting’
summary: Advice from Annie Lane.

title: Miss Manners: How do we respond to intrusive questions about our new adopted child?
summary: Advice from Judith Martin, Nicholas Ivor Martin and Jacobina Martin.

title: ADN Politics podcast: Snowplow politics, revisited
summary: In November, once again, Anchorage was hit with back-to-back snowstorms. And once again, the roads were a mess. For a winter city that relies on plowing so residents can get to work and schools can open, snow plowing quickly becomes a political issue — especially with a mayoral race coming up.

title: ‘Teaming With Microbes’ podcast: The power of ramial wood chips in the garden
summary: This week Jeff and Jonathan break down the garden science behind ramial chipped wood.They discuss its French roots, how it’s made, as well as the benefits it provides for soil, insects and animals.

title: Precut cantaloupe linked to ‘severe’ salmonella outbreak, CDC says
summary: Malichita and Rudy brand whole cantaloupes were recalled, as were precut cantaloupe products at stores such as Trader Joe’s and Kroger.

title: Israeli airstrikes on Gaza resume after weeklong truce with Hamas
summary: Israel dropped leaflets over parts of southern Gaza urging people to leave their homes, suggesting it was preparing to widen its offensive.

title: Montana’s first-in-the-nation ban on TikTok blocked by judge who calls it unconstitutional
summary: A sweeping ban of the video app, pushed by one of America’s least-populated states, was widely watched as a bellwether for future regulation. A federal judge said the law “violates the Constitution in more ways than one.”

title: U.S. stops helping Big Tech spot foreign meddling amid GOP legal threats
summary: The federal government has stopped warning Meta about foreign influence campaigns amid a legal campaign against the Biden administration’s communication with tech firms.

title: Anchorage teen’s accused killer on trial 45 years after her death
summary: Jurors heard opening arguments this week in the murder trial of defendant Donald McQuade, who is accused of killing 16-year-old Shelley Connolly in 1978.

title: What if Donald Trump is convicted? The 2024 Republican convention rules don’t address the issue.
summary: Trump is under four criminal indictments that will proceed through the GOP primary season, but the RNC rules don’t include any provisions addressing what happens if the party’s presumptive nominee is convicted of a felony.

title: Aid is trickling in, but Gaza still grows hungrier
summary: The World Food Program warns that the besieged enclave, where fighting was paused in part to allow the delivery of more humanitarian relief, remains at “risk of famine and starvation.”
